courses.title: Matrix Algebra [10.0 credits]
courses.description: Matrix algebra covering eigenvalues/eigenvectors, canonical/Jordan forms, QR/SVD, matrix norms, perturbation theory, positive/stochastic matrices, Lyapunov stability, and matrix equations; course expected to be given March - June 2026 at KTH.

courses.title: Convex Optimization with Engineering Applications [6.0 credits]
courses.description: Convex sets/functions, LP/QP/geometric/SDP, duality, interior-point and decomposition methods, with applications in estimation, data fitting, control, and communications; course expected to be given March - May 2026 at KTH.

courses.title: Great Ideas in Learning and Control [5.0 credits]
courses.description: Explores foundational 20th-century concepts in ML and control theory: information theory, dynamical systems, extremum problems, feedback, statistical learning, and dynamic programming; course expected to be given February - March 2026 at Uppsala University.

courses.title: Context Engineering for Football [10.0 credits]
courses.description: Explores techniques for obtaining reliable output from LLMs and communicating data accessibly, based on building Twelve Earpiece; covers transformer architectures and their implications for LLM usage in software applications, with hands-on app development as exam; course expected to start February 23rd 2026 at Uppsala University.

courses.title: Introduction to Network Science [3.0 credits]
courses.description: Network science covering basic concepts including centrality measures, network properties, network models, propagation dynamics, and network mining tasks (community detection, link prediction, position/role detection), with practical activities using R and network analysis software, plus student-selected advanced topics such as multilayer networks and temporal networks; course expected to take place May - June 2026 at Uppsala University.

courses.title: Spectral Graph Theory [7.5 credits]
courses.description: Course on spectral graph theory covering eigenvalues and eigenvectors of adjacency and Laplacian matrices, with applications to random walks, graph expansion, conductance, and energy minimization of Hamiltonian systems. Includes recent breakthroughs in community detection using non-backtracking matrices. Course expected to take place April 2026 at Uppsala University. Contact responsible fiona.skerman@math.uu.se for more information.

courses.title: Multivariable Control [8.0 credits]
courses.description: Introduction to robust linear multivariable control, covering feedback limitations, stability/performance under uncertainty, H2/H∞ methods, μ-synthesis, loop shaping, and LMIs; course expected to be given (for the last time (ever!)) in P4 of 2027 at KTH.

courses.title: Numerical Linear Algebra [7.5 credits]
courses.description: Numerical linear algebra methods for large-scale eigenvalue problems, linear systems, matrix functions and equations, with convergence theory, implementation aspects, and a project component; course expected to be given sometime in the fall of 2026 at KTH.

courses.title: Computational Game Theory [8.0 credits]
courses.description: Computational game theory covering Nash and Bayesian equilibria, stochastic and evolutionary games, coalition games, and mechanism design, with focus on formalizing, analyzing, and solving problems; course expected to be given sometime in the fall of 2027 at KTH.
